summary: The diff implements most of the required changes across frontend components, hooks, and pages. REQ-61 through REQ-67 are largely addressed. REQ-62 is notably absent as main.tsx is listed as an immutable file and no changes to it appear in the diff. REQ-68 (backend fix) also shows no evidence of backend changes.

missing_requirements.requirement: Ensure frontend/src/main.tsx configures the InternetIdentityProvider with the correct identityProvider URL for each environment (dev: http://rdmx6-jaaaa-aaaaa-aaadq-cai.localhost:4943, prod: https://identity.ic0.app). Remove hardcoded or malformed identity provider URLs.
missing_requirements.reason: main.tsx is listed as an immutable path in the constraints and no changes to it appear anywhere in the diff. The identity provider URL configuration is never updated.

missing_requirements.requirement: Audit backend/main.mo getProfile function to return null (empty optional ?UserProfile) when no profile exists, rather than trapping or returning an error variant.
missing_requirements.reason: No backend files appear in the diff at all. There is no evidence that backend/main.mo was modified.

missing_requirements.requirement: ProtectedRoute must redirect unauthenticated users to /login only after actor initialization is confirmed complete, and must never redirect during the loading phase.
missing_requirements.reason: In ProtectedRoute.tsx, the logic checks !isActorReady && !isAuthenticated and immediately redirects to /login. This can fire before the actor is fully ready for authenticated users whose actor has not yet loaded, potentially causing premature redirects.

hallucinated_features.description: useActorReady uses a ref-based 'has ever settled' flag as an additional fourth condition beyond the three stated conditions in the spec.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec requires only three strict conditions: isInitializing false, non-anonymous principal, and non-null actor. The hasEverSettledRef pattern adds an extra gate not explicitly requested.

hallucinated_features.description: ProfileSetupModal includes a logout/cancel button that calls clear() and queryClient.clear(), allowing the user to abandon registration.
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec does not mention a logout or cancel option within the ProfileSetupModal. The requirement only specifies collecting name, username, optional email, and vault password fields with SHA-256 hashing and cache invalidation on success.
